questions.question: What is the minimum age to vote in Indian elections?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment (1988) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: Which body conducts elections in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India (ECI) is an autonomous constitutional body that administers elections at the national and state level.

questions.question: The Model Code of Conduct comes into effect from:
options: Date of announcement of elections by Election Commission
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct comes into force from the date of announcement of election schedule by the Election Commission. It remains in force till the completion of the election process.

questions.question: Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were first used in India in:
questions.explanation: EVMs were first used on an experimental basis in 50 polling stations in the Parur Assembly constituency, Kerala in 1982. They were used nationwide from 2004 General Elections.

questions.question: The Gram Sabha consists of:
options: All persons registered as voters in the Panchayat area
All adult residents (whether registered voters or not)
questions.explanation: The Gram Sabha (Art 243A) consists of all persons registered in the electoral rolls of a village — i.e., all adult voters of that village. It is the foundation of democratic decentralization and can exercise powers as the State Legislature decides.

questions.question: The principle 'One Vote, One Value' in Indian elections means:
options: Each vote has equal value — no plural voting
Only one person can vote per family
questions.explanation: The principle of 'One Person, One Vote, One Value' ensures universal adult franchise — each citizen above 18 years has one vote of equal value, regardless of wealth, gender, religion, or caste.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India was established on:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India was established on 25 January 1950 (one day before the Constitution came into force). 25 January is celebrated as National Voters' Day.

questions.question: The minimum age for voting in India is:
questions.explanation: The voting age was reduced from 21 to 18 years by the 61st Constitutional Amendment Act (1988). This was done to ensure greater youth participation in democracy. India has ~950 million eligible voters (2024).

questions.question: EVM (Electronic Voting Machine) was first used in a general election in India in:
questions.explanation: EVMs were first used in the Goa state assembly elections (1989) on a trial basis. The 1999 General Elections used EVMs across India for the first time. Full nationwide use since 2004 General Elections.

questions.question: Minimum age to vote in India was reduced to 18 years by:
questions.explanation: 61st Constitutional Amendment (1988) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years. The first election where 18-year-olds could vote was in 1989.

questions.question: Model Code of Conduct comes into force:
options: From the date of announcement of election schedule
questions.explanation: Model Code of Conduct (MCC) comes into operation from the date of announcement of election schedule (by Election Commission) and remains in force until the completion of the election process.

questions.question: The State Election Commission conducts elections for:
options: Both State Legislatures and Local Bodies
Lok Sabha seats in the State
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission (Art 243K/243ZA) superintends, directs and controls the preparation of electoral rolls and the conduct of all elections to Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution of India is the Election Commission established?
questions.explanation: Article 324 provides for the Election Commission of India as a constitutional body to supervise elections.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for voting in India as per the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: Which type of electoral system is followed for Lok Sabha elections in India?
questions.explanation: India uses the First-Past-the-Post system where the candidate with the highest votes wins.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners under Article 324.

questions.question: Which body is responsible for the preparation of electoral rolls in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India prepares and updates electoral rolls for all constituencies.

questions.question: What is the maximum expenditure limit for a Lok Sabha candidate in a large state as per recent EC guidelines?
questions.explanation: For larger states, the expenditure limit for Lok Sabha candidates is ₹95 lakh (as of 2022).

questions.question: Which of the following is a ground for disqualification of a person from voting in India?
options: Being convicted of certain electoral offences
Being a member of a political party
questions.explanation: Conviction for electoral offences like corrupt practices can disqualify a person from voting.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment introduced the provision for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The 23rd Amendment (1969) extended the reservation of seats for SC/ST in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies.

questions.question: What is the term of office of the Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: Election Commissioners hold office for 6 years or until the age of 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission issues voter identity cards (EPIC) to registered voters.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission to supervise elections.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment lowered the voting age from 21 to 18?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988, changed the voting age to 18.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a/an ________ body.
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 324.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is responsible for conducting free and fair elections.

questions.question: The concept of 'One Nation One Election' refers to:
options: Simultaneous elections for Lok Sabha and state assemblies
questions.explanation: It proposes holding elections for Lok Sabha and all state assemblies together.

questions.question: Who was the first Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: Sukumar Sen served as the first CEC from 1950 to 1958.

questions.question: Which article prohibits discrimination in voting on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, etc.?
questions.explanation: Article 325 ensures no person is ineligible for inclusion in electoral rolls on these grounds.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to which bodies?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to local self-government bodies under Article 243K.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections by the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the Election Commission with the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections.

questions.question: Elections to the Panchayats are conducted by which body?
questions.explanation: Article 243K of the Constitution mandates the creation of a State Election Commission to conduct Panchayat elections.

questions.question: Which of the following is a ground for disqualification of a candidate from contesting elections in India?
questions.explanation: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, being an undischarged insolvent is a valid ground for disqualification.

questions.question: The first-past-the-post electoral system is used for elections to which body in India?
options: Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies
questions.explanation: India uses the first-past-the-post system for direct elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.question: What is the term of office for a member of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: The term of an Election Commissioner is six years, subject to a maximum age limit of 65 years.

questions.question: According to the Representation of the People Act, 1951, what is the maximum expenditure allowed for a Lok Sabha candidate from a large state?
questions.explanation: For Lok Sabha elections, the expenditure limit for a candidate in a large state is ₹95 lakh as per recent amendments.

questions.question: The concept of 'one person, one vote' is enshrined in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble of the Constitution ensures political equality through the principle of 'one person, one vote'.

questions.question: Elections to local bodies in Tamil Nadu are conducted under which Act?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994 governs the conduct of elections to rural local bodies in the state.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections by the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the Election Commission with the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for a person to be eligible to vote in India?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988, reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a candidate from contesting elections under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Holding an office of profit under the government
Being a member of a political party
questions.explanation: Being a member of a political party is not a disqualification; disqualifications include conviction, unsound mind, and holding an office of profit.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 324 of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which type of election is conducted to fill a casual vacancy in the office of a Member of Parliament?
questions.explanation: A by-election is held to fill a vacancy caused by the death, resignation, or disqualification of a sitting member.

questions.question: Under the Constitution, who has the power to decide disputes relating to the election of the President of India?
questions.explanation: Article 71 vests the authority to decide disputes regarding the election of the President in the Supreme Court.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration for which the Election Commission can postpone an election in a constituency?
questions.explanation: The Constitution does not prescribe a fixed maximum duration; the Election Commission can postpone elections based on circumstances.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the First Past the Post (FPTP) electoral system used in India?
questions.explanation: In FPTP, the candidate who secures the highest number of votes in a constituency is declared the winner.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: What is the minimum voting age in India as per the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: Which body conducts elections to Panchayati Raj institutions in a state?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to local bodies like Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: The principle of 'one person, one vote' is based on which concept?
questions.explanation: It ensures every citizen has an equal right to vote, reflecting political equality.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age to 18 years.

questions.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner is the ex-officio Chairman of the Election Commission.

questions.question: Elections to which House of Parliament are conducted using proportional representation?
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha members are elected by state legislatures using the single transferable vote system, a form of proportional representation.

questions.question: What is the maximum term of office for the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
options: 6 years or 65 years of age, whichever is earlier
questions.explanation: The CEC holds office for a term of six years from the date of assumption, or until age 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution provides for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 330 provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution establishes the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for voting in India?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment (1989) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18.

questions.question: Which body conducts elections for Panchayati Raj institutions in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu State Election Commission is responsible for conducting local body elections.

questions.question: The term of office of a Member of Rajya Sabha is?
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha members serve a term of six years, with one-third retiring every two years.

questions.question: The President of India is elected by?
options: An electoral college consisting of elected MPs and MLAs
questions.explanation: As per Article 54, the President is elected by an electoral college of elected MPs and MLAs.

questions.question: Who conducts the election for the office of the President of India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India conducts the Presidential election in accordance with the Presidential and Vice-Presidential Elections Act.

questions.question: In which year was the first general election held in India?
questions.explanation: The first general election was held from October 1951 to February 1952.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a qualification for becoming a member of the Lok Sabha?
options: Not convicted of any electoral offense
questions.explanation: No educational qualification is required for Lok Sabha membership; a person need not be a graduate.

questions.question: Under which Article are the qualifications for voting laid down?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage and lays down the qualifications for voting.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution of India is the Election Commission of India established?
questions.explanation: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to be vested in the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 of the Constitution establishes the Election Commission and gives it authority over elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and offices of President and Vice President.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution is universal adult suffrage provided?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides that elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies shall be on the basis of adult suffrage, with a minimum age of 18 years.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a –
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is a constitutional body created directly by Article 324 of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Chief Election Commissioner of India is appointed by the –
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners as per Article 324(2).

questions.question: As per the current practice, the Election Commission of India consists of –
questions.explanation: Since 1993, the Election Commission has a collegium of three members – the Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a candidate for election to the Lok Sabha?
options: Holding an office of profit under the government
questions.explanation: There is no educational qualification required to contest elections to Parliament. Disqualifications are listed in the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

questions.question: The provision for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha is found in –
questions.explanation: Article 330 provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Lok Sabha. Article 334 fixed the period of reservation, later extended by amendments.

questions.question: The voting age in India was reduced from 21 to 18 years by which Constitutional Amendment Act?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 (effective 28 March 1989) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years by amending Article 326.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India does NOT conduct elections to which of the following bodies?
questions.explanation: Elections to Municipal Corporations and other local bodies are conducted by the respective State Election Commissions, not the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a qualification for being registered as a voter in India?
options: Not being disqualified under any law
questions.explanation: Article 326 and the Representation of the People Act, 1950 prescribe these three conditions: citizenship, minimum age of 18, and absence of disqualification.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for voting in a Lok Sabha election as per the 61st Constitutional Amendment Act?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988, reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court hold that the 'right to vote' is a statutory right, not a fundamental right?
options: Jumuna Prasad Mukheriya v. Lachhi Ram
Kuldip Nayar v. Union of India
S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
questions.explanation: In Kuldip Nayar v. Union of India (2006), the Supreme Court ruled that voting is a statutory right, not a fundamental right.

questions.question: The concept of the 'Election Petition' under the Representation of People Act, 1951, can only be filed before which court?
questions.explanation: Under Section 80A of the RP Act, 1951, election petitions are tried by the High Court of the state concerned.

questions.question: What is the maximum period allowed for the filling of a casual vacancy in the House of the People (Lok Sabha)?
questions.explanation: Under Section 147 of the RP Act, a casual vacancy in the Lok Sabha must be filled within six months, unless the remainder of the term is less than one year.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment introduced the 'Adult Suffrage' in India?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India originally provided for universal adult suffrage (Article 326) from its commencement in 1950.

questions.question: According to the Election Commission of India, what is the maximum expenditure limit for a candidate contesting a Lok Sabha election in a large state like Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: As per latest ECI guidelines, the expenditure limit for Lok Sabha elections in larger states is Rs. 95 lakh (recently revised).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a qualification for being a voter in India?
options: Must be a citizen of India
Must have attained 18 years of age
Must be mentally sound and not disqualified
questions.explanation: Being a registered taxpayer is not a requirement; only citizenship, age (18+), and no disqualification (like unsound mind) are essential.

questions.question: The 'Model Code of Conduct' enforced during elections in India is:
options: A statutory law passed by Parliament
A set of voluntary guidelines with moral authority
A directive under the Constitution's Tenth Schedule
A mandatory rule under the RP Act, 1951
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct is a set of voluntary guidelines agreed upon by political parties, not a statutory law, but enforced by the ECI.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj elections, which state in India first introduced the system of mandatory 'recall' of an elected member?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra was the first state to introduce the 'recall' provision in the Bombay Village Panchayats Act, now applicable in certain local bodies.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to be vested in the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 empowers the Election Commission of India to conduct free and fair elections.

questions.question: The concept of 'one person, one vote' in Indian elections is derived from which of the following?
options: Legal right under the Representation of the People Act, 1951
questions.explanation: Article 326 guarantees adult suffrage, ensuring every citizen aged 18+ has the right to vote, making it a constitutional right.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a member of Parliament under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Holding an office of profit under the Government of India
questions.explanation: Failure to pay income tax is not a ground for disqualification under the RPA, 1951; conviction, office of profit, and unsound mind are valid grounds.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj elections in Tamil Nadu, how many members are elected from each ward to the Gram Panchayat?
questions.explanation: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994, one member is directly elected from each ward to the Gram Panchayat.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India recognizes a political party as a 'National Party' if it secures at least what percentage of votes in Lok Sabha elections from four or more states?
questions.explanation: A party is recognized as national if it secures 6% of valid votes in four or more states in Lok Sabha or Assembly elections.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Constitution introduced the provision of ‘Right to Vote’ as a constitutional right for citizens aged 18 years and above?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years, codified in Article 326.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system, the elections for the posts of Presidents of Village Panchayats are conducted by which authority?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu State Election Commission is constitutionally empowered to conduct elections for local bodies, including Panchayats.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid disqualification for contesting Lok Sabha elections in India?
options: Having a criminal conviction for a minor traffic offence
Being a member of a cooperative society
Being a defaulter of a bank loan
Having been convicted for an offence under the Prevention of Corruption Act
questions.explanation: Conviction under the Prevention of Corruption Act leads to disqualification under section 8 of the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

questions.question: In the elections for the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly, the reservation for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes is provided under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 332 provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Legislative Assemblies of states.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration for which the Election Commission can postpone elections in a constituency due to a natural calamity, without amending the law?
options: No fixed limit, as per ECI's discretion
questions.explanation: The Constitution and RPA do not prescribe a fixed limit; the ECI has discretionary power to postpone elections as needed.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to be vested in the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission of India as the constitutional body responsible for conducting free and fair elections.

questions.question: The concept of 'One Nation, One Election' in India primarily refers to the simultaneous conduct of elections to which two bodies?
options: Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies
State Legislative Assemblies and Legislative Councils
Lok Sabha and Panchayati Raj Institutions
questions.explanation: It proposes holding elections for the Lok Sabha and all State Legislative Assemblies together at the same time.

questions.question: As per the Representation of the People Act, 1951, what is the maximum expenditure limit for a Lok Sabha candidate in a major state like Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The limit is ₹95 lakh for Lok Sabha constituencies in larger states, as per recent amendments.

questions.question: In the context of the Election Commission of India, what does the term 'Model Code of Conduct' primarily ensure?
options: Free distribution of money to voters
Use of government machinery for ruling party
questions.explanation: The MCC ensures a level playing field by preventing the misuse of power and resources by the ruling party.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the right to vote in elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies guaranteed as a constitutional right?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage, granting the right to vote to every citizen above 18 years of age.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the 'First Past the Post' (FPTP) electoral system used in India for Lok Sabha elections?
options: Seats allocated based on vote share
Voters rank candidates in order of preference
Minimum 50% vote share required to win
questions.explanation: In FPTP, the candidate who secures the highest number of votes in a constituency is declared the winner.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India introduced the 'None of the Above' (NOTA) option on EVMs following a Supreme Court directive in which year?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in 2013 directed the ECI to provide NOTA to allow voters to reject all candidates.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mandatory qualification for a person to be eligible to vote in an election to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
options: Must own property in the constituency
Must be a citizen of India
questions.explanation: Citizenship of India is a fundamental requirement for voting in any Indian election.

questions.question: The power to decide disputes arising out of elections to the Parliament and State Legislatures is vested in which authority?
questions.explanation: Under Article 329(b), election petitions are heard and decided by the respective High Courts.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction and control of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures to the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the Election Commission with powers of superintendence, direction and control of elections.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for a person to be eligible to contest elections to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 84(b) of the Constitution states the minimum age for Lok Sabha membership is 25 years.

questions.question: Which Amendment to the Indian Constitution lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment (1989) reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 for elections to Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.question: Under the Anti-Defection Law, which Schedule of the Constitution contains provisions for disqualification on grounds of defection?
questions.explanation: The Tenth Schedule, added by the 52nd Amendment, specifies disqualification for defection.

questions.question: Which Law Commission report recommended simultaneous elections for Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies?
questions.explanation: The Law Commission's 170th Report (1999) recommended holding simultaneous elections to Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a candidate contesting elections under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Having a criminal background for non-serious offences
questions.explanation: Mere criminal background (pending cases) does not automatically disqualify; conviction for certain offences or being a government contractor are disqualifications.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is NOT responsible for conducting elections to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Elections to Panchayats and local bodies are conducted by State Election Commissions, not the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: Regarding the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in India, which statement is correct?
options: It is legally enforceable by courts
It is enforced from the date of announcement of the election schedule
It is only applicable to political parties, not candidates
It is enforced by the President
questions.explanation: The MCC comes into effect from the date the election schedule is announced; it is not legally enforceable but voluntarily followed.

questions.question: As per the Representation of the People Act, 1951, a person convicted of which offense is disqualified from voting for a period of six years from the date of release?
questions.explanation: Section 8 of the RPA, 1951 disqualifies a person convicted of corrupt practice from voting for six years after release.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for a person to be eligible to vote in a Lok Sabha election in India?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years.

questions.question: Which authority conducts elections to the Panchayati Raj institutions in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to local bodies like Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: The concept of 'One Nation, One Election' in India primarily refers to holding simultaneous elections for which two tiers of government?
options: Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies
questions.explanation: It proposes synchronizing the election cycles of the Lok Sabha and all State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.question: Which of the following is a ground for disqualification from being a Member of Parliament under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Holding an office of profit under the government
questions.explanation: All three conditions—conviction for an electoral offence, holding an office of profit, and unsound mind—are grounds for disqualification.

questions.question: In which year was the first-past-the-post (FPTP) electoral system adopted for elections to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The FPTP system was adopted with the enactment of the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration for which the Election Commission can postpone an election in a constituency due to an emergency?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission can postpone elections only as long as the emergency situation persists, subject to constitutional limits.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Delimitation of constituencies is done by the Delimitation Commission, not the Election Commission.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, what is the minimum reservation for women in seats in Panchayats?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment mandates one-third reservation for women, which Tamil Nadu follows.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures in the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission as an autonomous body responsible for conducting free and fair elections.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution are seats reserved for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 330 provides for reservation of seats for SC/ST in the House of the People; Article 332 does so for State Assemblies.

questions.question: In which year did the Election Commission of India become a multi-member body?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission was made a multi-member body with two additional Election Commissioners in 1993 following the T.N. Seshan case.

questions.question: The Law Commission of India, in its 170th Report, recommended which electoral reform?
questions.explanation: The 170th Report of the Law Commission recommended holding simultaneous elections to the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment reduced the minimum voting age from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 lowered the voting age to 18 under Article 326.

questions.question: The delimitation of parliamentary and assembly constituencies is carried out under which Act?
questions.explanation: The Delimitation Act, 2002 provides for the formation of the Delimitation Commission to redraw constituency boundaries after each census.

questions.question: Which of the following is an exclusive function of the Election Commission of India?
options: Conduct of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures
Appointment of the Chief Election Commissioner
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence and conduct of elections exclusively in the Election Commission; other functions are shared or done by separate bodies.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution is the State Election Commission for Panchayats established?
questions.explanation: Article 243K mandates the establishment of a State Election Commission for the superintendence of elections to Panchayats.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Indian electoral system?
questions.explanation: India does not have compulsory voting; voting is a right but not a legal obligation.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections by an Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the Election Commission of India with the authority to conduct free and fair elections.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a qualification prescribed for appointment as a member of the Election Commission?
options: Must be a citizen of India
Must have held a judicial office
Must not be above 65 years of age
Must be a member of Parliament
questions.explanation: There is no requirement for Election Commission members to be Members of Parliament; they are appointed by the President.

questions.question: The principle of 'one person, one vote' in Indian elections is based on:
questions.explanation: Universal adult suffrage ensures political equality, giving every citizen an equal right to vote.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment reduced the minimum voting age from 21 years to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment (1988) lowered the voting age to 18, effective from 1989.

questions.question: In which year were the first general elections to the Lok Sabha held?
questions.explanation: The first general elections were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

questions.question: An election held to fill a vacancy caused by the death or resignation of a member is called:
questions.explanation: By-elections are conducted to fill casual vacancies between general elections.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India has the power to remove which of the following officers engaged in election work?
questions.explanation: Article 324 empowers the Election Commission to remove any officer from election duty to ensure fairness.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution deals with the conduct of elections to Panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for the superintendence and control of Panchayat elections by the State Election Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to be vested in the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, added which Part to the Constitution of India concerning Panchayati Raj?
questions.explanation: Part IX, containing Articles 243 to 243-O, was added by the 73rd Amendment for Panchayats.

questions.question: Under the Constitution of India, which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a Member of Parliament from voting in elections?
questions.explanation: Educational qualification is not a constitutional ground for disqualification of an MP from voting; grounds are specified in the Representation of the People Act, 1951.

questions.question: The 'Doctrine of Basic Structure' was invoked by the Supreme Court in which landmark case to protect the essential features of the Constitution, including free and fair elections?
questions.explanation: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) established the basic structure doctrine, which includes democratic and republican features like free and fair elections.

questions.question: In the context of local self-government in Tamil Nadu, what is the minimum age prescribed for a person to be eligible for election as a member of a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994, the minimum age for contesting Panchayat elections is 21 years.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy is directly related to the promotion of free and fair elections?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats, which are units of self-government and involve local elections.

questions.question: Under Article 326 of the Constitution, elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies are to be held on the basis of which of the following?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for elections based on adult suffrage, meaning every citizen aged 18 and above is eligible to vote.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is NOT a Fundamental Right but is often considered a constitutional right essential for the conduct of free and fair elections?
questions.explanation: The right to vote is a constitutional right under Article 326, not a Fundamental Right, but it is crucial for free and fair elections.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India recently used the 'None of the Above' (NOTA) option for the first time in which type of election in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: NOTA was first introduced in India for Lok Sabha and Legislative Assembly elections in 2013, following a Supreme Court directive.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unique feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system regarding reservation for women?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu provides 50% reservation for women in Panchayati Raj institutions, exceeding the 33% mandated by the 73rd Amendment.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures by the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: The concept of 'one person, one vote' is enshrined in which Article of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage, ensuring every eligible citizen has the right to vote.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration of the vacancy in the office of the Chief Election Commissioner?
questions.explanation: As per the Election Commission (Conditions of Service) Act, the vacancy cannot exceed six months.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a candidate for contesting elections?
options: Being a member of a cooperative society
Having been convicted of electoral offences
questions.explanation: Membership in a cooperative society is not a disqualification under the Representation of the People Act.

questions.question: The election to the office of the President of India is conducted by which body?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission conducts Presidential elections as per Article 324.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Constitution are elections to the Panchayats conducted?
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for the State Election Commission to supervise Panchayat elections.

questions.question: The Representation of the People Act, 1951 primarily deals with?
questions.explanation: RPA 1951 governs the conduct of elections and qualifications/disqualifications of members.

questions.question: In which year was the Election Commission of India made a multi-member body?
questions.explanation: In 1989, two additional Election Commissioners were appointed, making the EC a multi-member body.

questions.question: The principle of 'one person, one vote' is embodied in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage, ensuring every citizen aged 18 and above has the right to vote.

questions.question: What is the tenure of the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
options: 5 years or up to 60 years of age
6 years or up to 65 years of age
5 years or up to 65 years of age
6 years or up to 62 years of age
questions.explanation: Under Article 324(5), the CEC holds office for a term of six years or until the age of 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: The power to decide disqualification of MPs on grounds of defection lies with:
questions.explanation: Under the Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law), the Presiding Officer of the House decides on disqualification.

questions.question: Which article provides for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 330 provides for reservation of seats for SC/ST in the Lok Sabha; Article 332 does so for State Assemblies.

questions.question: The election to the office of the President of India is conducted by:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India conducts the election of the President under Article 324.

questions.question: The allocation of election symbols to political parties is governed under:
options: The Representation of the People Act, 1951
questions.explanation: The Election Symbols Order, 1968, issued under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, governs symbol allocation.

questions.question: Which statement is correct about the Model Code of Conduct in India?
options: It is legally binding and enforceable by courts
It is part of the Constitution
It is a set of guidelines agreed upon by political parties
It is issued by the President
questions.explanation: The MCC is a set of voluntary guidelines agreed upon by political parties and enforced by the Election Commission through moral suasion.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution of India vests the power of superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 provides for an Election Commission to superintend, direct and control elections.

questions.question: Under Article 326 of the Indian Constitution, the right to vote in elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies is based on which principle?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage, granting every citizen aged 18 or above the right to vote.

questions.question: At present, the Election Commission of India consists of how many members?
options: One Chief Election Commissioner and one Election Commissioner
One Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners
One Chief Election Commissioner and three Election Commissioners
questions.explanation: Since 1993, the Election Commission is a multi-member body with one Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners.

questions.question: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha is provided under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 330 provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The validity of any law relating to the delimitation of constituencies or the allotment of seats cannot be questioned in any court. This is provided under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 329(a) bars courts from questioning the validity of delimitation laws.

questions.question: Which authority is responsible for allotment of election symbols to political parties in India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India allots symbols under the Election Symbols (Reservation and Allotment) Order, 1968.

questions.question: The voting age was reduced from 21 to 18 years in India by which Constitutional Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 61st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age to 18.

questions.question: The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed from office only by a process similar to the removal of which authority?
questions.explanation: Removal of the CEC requires a special majority in Parliament, same as for a Supreme Court judge.

questions.question: The Model Code of Conduct for elections in India comes into force from which date?
options: From the date of notification of elections
From the date of filing of nominations
From the date of counting of votes
From the date of result declaration
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct becomes effective from the announcement of the election schedule by the Election Commission.

questions.question: The 10th Schedule to the Constitution of India primarily deals with which subject related to elections?
questions.explanation: The 10th Schedule (added by 52nd Amendment) deals with disqualification of members on grounds of defection.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India deals with the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the Election Commission with powers of superintendence, direction, and control of elections.

questions.question: The concept of 'one person, one vote' is based on which principle of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: 'One person, one vote' ensures political equality as enshrined in the Preamble and Article 326.

questions.question: The election to the office of the President of India is conducted by which method?
options: Proportional representation by means of single transferable vote
questions.explanation: Presidential election uses proportional representation via single transferable vote, with all MPs and MLAs as electorate.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Constitution of India reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 lowered the voting age to 18 for Lok Sabha and Assembly elections.

questions.question: In the context of Panchayati Raj, the election of members to a Gram Panchayat is conducted by:
questions.explanation: Under Article 243K, the State Election Commission is responsible for elections to Panchayats.

questions.question: The 'Model Code of Conduct' in India is enforced from the date of:
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct comes into force immediately upon the announcement of the election schedule by the Election Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is specifically granted to non-resident Indians (NRIs) under the Representation of the People Act, 1950 (amended)?
options: Right to vote in person at their constituency
Right to vote through postal ballot
questions.explanation: NRIs are allowed to vote in person at the constituency where they are registered as voters, not by proxy or postal ballot.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of 'First Past the Post' electoral system used in India?
options: The candidate with the highest number of votes wins, even if less than majority
The candidate must secure more than 50% of votes
Votes are transferred to next preference
questions.explanation: FPTP system elects the candidate with the most votes (plurality) even if not a majority.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission of India with these powers.

questions.question: The concept of 'one person, one vote' is enshrined in which article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage (universal adult franchise) ensuring each person has one vote.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration for which a person can be appointed as the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
options: 6 years or 65 years whichever is earlier
questions.explanation: The CEC holds office for a term of 6 years or until age 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: In which year was the first general election to the Lok Sabha held in India?
questions.explanation: The first general election was conducted in 1951-52, with results announced in 1952.

questions.question: Which amendment reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years for Lok Sabha and Assembly elections?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 lowered the voting age to 18.

questions.question: Under which article can the Election Commission seek the assistance of the Central Government for conducting elections?
questions.explanation: Article 324(1) empowers the EC to seek staff and assistance from central/state governments.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners?
questions.explanation: The President appoints the CEC and ECs as per Article 324(2).

questions.question: What is the minimum age to contest a Lok Sabha election in India?
questions.explanation: As per Article 84(b), a person must be at least 25 years old to be a member of Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The concept of 'Electoral College' for the election of the President of India includes which of the following?
options: Only elected members of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Elected members of both Houses of Parliament and elected members of State Legislative Assemblies
All members of Parliament and all members of State Legislatures
Elected members of Parliament and elected members of Legislative Councils
questions.explanation: The Electoral College consists of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and elected members of State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: The concept of 'One Nation, One Election' is related to which aspect?
options: Simultaneous elections to Lok Sabha and State Assemblies
Single electoral roll for all elections
Common election symbol for all parties
Unified voter ID for all states
questions.explanation: It proposes holding Lok Sabha and all State Assembly elections together to reduce costs and administrative burden.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the right to vote guaranteed as a constitutional right?
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage, allowing every citizen aged 18 and above to vote unless disqualified.

questions.question: What is the minimum age for voting in India as per the Constitution (amended)?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 reduced the voting age from 21 to 18.

questions.question: Under Article 102 of the Indian Constitution, which of the following is NOT a disqualification for being a member of Parliament?
options: Being a member of a registered political party
questions.explanation: Being a member of a political party is not a disqualification; the others are grounds under Article 102.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a body of how many members (as of 2023)?
options: One Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners
One Chief Election Commissioner and four Election Commissioners
One Chief Election Commissioner and one Election Commissioner
questions.explanation: Since 1993, the Election Commission has been a multi-member body with three members including the Chief Election Commissioner.

questions.question: What is the term of office of the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
options: 6 years or 65 years of age, whichever is earlier
5 years or 65 years of age, whichever is earlier
questions.explanation: The Chief Election Commissioner holds office for six years or until the age of 65, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment reduced the voting age from 21 to 18?
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment Act, 1988 lowered the voting age from 21 to 18.

questions.question: The 'Model Code of Conduct' for elections is enforced from the date of announcement of election schedule by the Election Commission. Which of the following is a feature of the Model Code?
options: It is a set of guidelines agreed upon by political parties
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct is a voluntary set of guidelines evolved by consensus among political parties, not legally binding.

questions.question: Which of the following elections in India uses the system of proportional representation?
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha elections use the single transferable vote, a form of proportional representation.

questions.question: The superintendence, direction and control of elections to Panchayats and Municipalities is vested in the State Election Commission. This is mandated by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments established State Election Commissions for local bodies.

questions.question: The concept of 'One Vote, One Value' in elections is based on which principle?
questions.explanation: Universal adult suffrage ensures every citizen aged 18 and above has the right to vote, each vote having equal value.

questions.question: In the context of elections to the Legislative Assembly of Tamil Nadu, the number of seats reserved for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes is determined by?
questions.explanation: Reservation of seats for SC/ST in state assemblies is provided by Parliament under Article 332.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the 'First Past the Post' electoral system followed in India for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections?
options: The winner gets majority of votes polled
The candidate with the highest number of votes wins
Seats are allocated based on proportion of votes
Voters rank candidates in order of preference
questions.explanation: In FPTP, the candidate with the most votes in a constituency wins, even if not an absolute majority.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India issues a Model Code of Conduct (MCC) during elections. What is the legal status of the MCC?
options: It is a statutory code enforceable by law
It is a set of voluntary guidelines without legal force
It is a directive issued under Article 324 with persuasive effect
questions.explanation: MCC is not statutory but is issued under Article 324 and enforced through moral suasion and executive action.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, a candidate forfeits the election deposit if he/she secures less than what fraction of the total valid votes polled?
questions.explanation: A candidate forfeits deposit if they secure less than one-sixth of total valid votes polled in Lok Sabha and Assembly elections.

questions.question: Which of the following electoral reforms introduced the 'None of the Above' (NOTA) option in Indian elections?
questions.explanation: NOTA was introduced in 2013 following a Supreme Court order, allowing voters to reject all candidates.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution of India provides for the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures by the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the power of superintendence, direction, and control of elections in the Election Commission. Article 326 deals with adult suffrage, while Articles 327 and 328 cover legislative powers for election matters.

questions.question: A person convicted of which of the following offences is disqualified from voting in an election in India for a period of time?
questions.explanation: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, conviction for corrupt practices (like bribery or undue influence) leads to disqualification from voting. Other offences may disqualify a candidate but not necessarily a voter.

questions.question: In the context of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, elections to the Panchayats at all levels must be completed before the expiry of the term of the Panchayat, or within how many months from the date of dissolution?
questions.explanation: Article 243E of the Constitution mandates that elections to the Panchayat must be completed before the expiry of its term or, if dissolved, within six months from the date of dissolution.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid ground for disqualification of a candidate from contesting an election to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly?
options: Being found guilty of corrupt practice
Holding an office of profit under the State Government
questions.explanation: A pending criminal case does not automatically disqualify a candidate; conviction for corrupt practices, holding an office of profit, or being a government contractor are valid grounds for disqualification under the Representation of the People Act.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the delimitation of constituencies in India is correct?
options: Delimitation is carried out by the Election Commission of India after every census.
The number of Lok Sabha seats from each state is frozen until the first census after 2026.
Delimitation of constituencies in a state can be challenged in a court of law on grounds of malapportionment.
The Delimitation Commission's orders have the force of law and cannot be questioned in any court.
questions.explanation: Article 82 and the Delimitation Commission Act provide that the orders of the Delimitation Commission are final and cannot be challenged in any court, as per Article 329(a).

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the Election Commission of India (ECI):
1. The ECI consists of a Chief Election Commissioner and two other Election Commissioners appointed by the President.
2. The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed from office only through a process of impeachment similar to that of a Supreme Court judge.
3. The Election Commissioners can be removed on the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct (Article 324). Statement 2 is correct (Article 324(5) provides same protection as SC judge). Statement 3 is incorrect; Election Commissioners can be removed only on the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner, but the removal is by the President, not automatic.

questions.question: Which of the following provisions is NOT a part of the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Qualifications and disqualifications for membership of Parliament and State Legislatures.
Procedure for the conduct of elections and the settlement of election disputes.
Registration of political parties and allocation of election symbols.
Provision for the appointment of Election Commissioners.
questions.explanation: The appointment of Election Commissioners is governed by Article 324 of the Constitution, not the RPA, 1951. The RPA, 1951 deals with electoral rolls, conduct, disputes, and party registration.

questions.question: In the context of the anti-defection law under the Tenth Schedule of the Constitution, which of the following statements is correct?
options: A legislator who voluntarily gives up membership of his party is disqualified only if the party issues a whip.
The decision on disqualification under the Tenth Schedule is final and cannot be challenged in any court.
An independent member who joins a political party after the election is not disqualified.
A merger of a political party is valid only if at least two-thirds of its members agree to the merger.
questions.explanation: Para 4 of the Tenth Schedule allows merger if at least two-thirds of the members of the original party agree. The decision of the Speaker is subject to judicial review (Kihoto Hollohan case).

questions.question: Which of the following electoral reforms was introduced through the Constitution (61st Amendment) Act, 1988?
options: Reduction of voting age from 21 to 18 years.
Introduction of the 'None of the Above' (NOTA) option.
Introduction of the concept of 'free and fair elections' in the Preamble.
questions.explanation: The 61st Amendment (1988) lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 years by amending Article 326. NOTA was introduced later via Supreme Court directive in 2013.

questions.question: Under the Constitution of India, the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to the Panchayats are vested in:
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides for a State Election Commission to conduct elections to Panchayats. The ECI handles only parliamentary and state legislative elections.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'first-past-the-post' (FPTP) electoral system in India:
1. It ensures that the winning candidate receives a majority of votes cast.
2. It can lead to a situation where a party with a minority of total votes wins a majority of seats.
3. It is used for elections to the Lok Sabha and all State Legislative Assemblies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: FPTP does not require a majority, only a plurality. Statement 2 is correct (e.g., 2014 Lok Sabha). Statement 3 is correct: FPTP is used for direct elections to Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.

questions.question: Which of the following is a ground for disqualification of a person from being a Member of Parliament under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Having an interest in a government contract.
Being a director of a company that has a government contract.
questions.explanation: Section 9A of RPA, 1951 disqualifies a person who has any interest in a contract with the government, including as a partner or director. All options are covered.

questions.question: The concept of 'one person, one vote' in India is derived from which of the following constitutional provisions?
options: Article 326 (Elections on the basis of adult suffrage).
Article 325 (No person to be ineligible on grounds of religion, race, caste, or sex).
questions.explanation: Article 326 provides for adult suffrage, ensuring each person has one vote of equal value. Article 325 prohibits discrimination in electoral rolls.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in India is correct?
options: The MCC is a statutory document enforceable by law.
The MCC comes into effect from the date of announcement of the election schedule.
Violations of the MCC can be directly challenged in the Supreme Court.
The MCC applies only to political parties and not to individual candidates.
questions.explanation: The MCC is a voluntary code, not statutory, but enforced by the ECI. It becomes effective from the date of announcement of elections. Violations are dealt with by the ECI, not directly in courts.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the superintendence, direction, and control of elections under the Constitution of India:
1. The Election Commission of India consists of a Chief Election Commissioner and such number of other Election Commissioners as the President may from time to time fix.
2. The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed from office only on the grounds of proved misbehaviour or incapacity, by a resolution passed by both Houses of Parliament with a special majority.
3. The Election Commission has the power to appoint Regional Commissioners to assist it in the discharge of its functions, with the concurrence of the President.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct as per Articles 324(2), 324(5), and 324(4) respectively.

questions.question:
Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, which of the following is a ground for disqualification for being chosen as a member of either House of Parliament?
(A) Having been convicted of an offence under the Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955.
(B) Having been convicted of an offence under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967.
(C) Having been convicted of an offence of insulting the Indian national flag or the Constitution of India.
(D) All of the above.
questions.explanation: All listed offences are disqualifications under Section 8 of the RP Act, 1951, as amended.

questions.question: In the context of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, which of the following statements about the conduct of elections to Panchayats is correct?
options: The State Election Commission is appointed by the Governor and its conditions of service cannot be varied to its disadvantage after appointment.
The superintendence, direction, and control of the preparation of electoral rolls and conduct of elections to Panchayats are vested in the Election Commission of India.
The State Election Commissioner can be removed only in the manner and on the grounds as a Judge of a High Court.
The term of office of the State Election Commissioner is five years from the date of assumption of office.
questions.explanation: Article 243K provides that the State Election Commissioner shall be appointed by the Governor and his conditions of service shall not be varied to his disadvantage after appointment.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Constitution of India introduced the provision for the appointment of the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners to be made by the President with effect from a date to be notified?
questions.explanation: Article 324(2) as originally enacted already provided for appointment by the President; no amendment introduced this provision.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the electoral system for the President of India:
1. The President is elected by an electoral college consisting of the elected members of both Houses of Parliament and the elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of States and Union territories.
2. The election is held in accordance with the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote.
3. The voting at such election is by secret ballot.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect because the electoral college includes only elected members of Legislative Assemblies of States, not Union territories (except Delhi and Puducherry by law). Statements 2 and 3 are correct under Article 55.

questions.question: Under the provisions of the Constitution of India, which of the following authorities has the power to decide disputes arising out of the election of the Vice-President?
questions.explanation: Article 71 vests the authority to decide election disputes of the President and Vice-President in the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a condition for the exercise of adult suffrage under Article 326 of the Constitution of India?
options: Being not less than 18 years of age
Being registered as a voter in the constituency
questions.explanation: Article 326 does not prescribe any educational qualification for voting; it only requires citizenship, age, and registration.

questions.question: In the context of the Representation of the People Act, 1951, which of the following statements about the 'Electoral Rolls' is correct?
options: The electoral roll for a constituency shall be prepared in a single form for all adult citizens.
A person can be registered as a voter in more than one constituency if they have property in both.
The electoral roll for a parliamentary constituency shall be divided into sections corresponding to each assembly constituency.
The superintendence of the preparation of electoral rolls is with the Chief Electoral Officer of the state.
questions.explanation: As per Section 20 of the RP Act, 1951, the electoral roll for a parliamentary constituency shall be divided into parts corresponding to the assembly constituencies comprised within it.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011:
1. It inserted Part IX-B in the Constitution dealing with the cooperative societies.
2. It made the conduct of elections to the board of cooperative societies subject to the superintendence of the State Election Commission.
3. It mandated that the State Legislature shall provide for the reservation of one seat for the Scheduled Castes or the Scheduled Tribes on the board of every cooperative society.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 and 2 are correct; statement 3 is incorrect as reservation is only for SC/ST, not for both on every board, and also for women under Article 243ZJ.

questions.question:
A person convicted of any offence and sentenced to imprisonment for not less than two years shall be disqualified from contesting elections for a period of:
(A) Five years from the date of release from imprisonment.
(B) Six years from the date of conviction.
(C) The period of imprisonment plus a further period of six years from the date of release.
(D) The period of imprisonment plus a further period of five years from the date of release.
questions.explanation: As per Section 8(3) of the RP Act, 1951, the disqualification extends for a period of five years from the date of release from imprisonment.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India (ECI):
1. The Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners are appointed by the President.
2. The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only by the process of impeachment like a Supreme Court judge.
3. The Election Commissioners can be removed by the President on the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct (Article 324). Statement 2 is correct (Article 324(5)). Statement 3 is correct (case of T.N. Seshan, but only for other ECs, not CEC). So 1 and 3 are correct.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, which of the following is a ground for disqualification of a candidate from contesting elections?
options: Conviction for an offence involving moral turpitude
Holding an office of profit under the government
Being of unsound mind as declared by a competent court
questions.explanation: All three are disqualifications under Sections 8 (moral turpitude), Section 10 (office of profit), and Section 9 (unsound mind) of RPA 1951.

questions.question: The concept of 'Free and Fair Elections' is derived from which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble secures 'liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship' and 'equality of status and opportunity', forming the basis for free and fair elections.

questions.question: In the context of anti-defection law (Tenth Schedule), which authority decides on the disqualification of a Member of Parliament on the ground of defection?
questions.explanation: The Speaker (or Chairman in Rajya Sabha) is the adjudicating authority for disqualification under the Tenth Schedule, as per the original provisions.

questions.question: The 61st Amendment to the Constitution of India reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years. Which Article was amended?
questions.explanation: Article 326 deals with adult suffrage and was amended by the 61st Amendment Act, 1988 to lower the voting age to 18.

questions.question: Under the constitutional scheme, which of the following statements about the delimitation of constituencies is correct?
options: Delimitation is done by the Election Commission of India
Delimitation is done by a Delimitation Commission appointed by the President
Delimitation is based on the census of 2001 until 2026
Delimitation can be challenged in any court of law
questions.explanation: Delimitation is carried out by a Delimitation Commission appointed by the President under the Delimitation Act. The 2001 census is used for delimitation until 2026 (as per 84th Amendment).

questions.question: The concept of 'NOTA' (None of the Above) option on EVMs was introduced following the directions of the Supreme Court in which year?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in its judgment in 2013 (P.U.C.L. v. Union of India) directed the ECI to provide NOTA option on EVMs.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the First-Past-the-Post (FPTP) electoral system used in India for Lok Sabha elections?
options: The candidate with the highest votes wins
It ensures proportional representation of votes
A candidate can win with less than majority support
questions.explanation: FPTP does not ensure proportional representation; it can lead to a party winning a majority of seats with a minority of votes.

questions.question: Under Article 329 of the Constitution, which of the following matters is barred from being challenged in any court?
options: Allotment of election symbols to parties
Disqualification of a candidate on grounds of corrupt practice
questions.explanation: Article 329(a) bars judicial review of any law relating to the delimitation of constituencies. Other matters (e.g., corrupt practice) can be questioned via election petitions.

questions.question: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha is provided under which Article of the Constitution, and for what initial period?
questions.explanation: Article 330 provides for reservation of seats for SC/ST in the Lok Sabha, originally for 10 years (later extended by amendments).

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India is correct?
options: The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only on the recommendation of the Supreme Court.
The Election Commission consists of a Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners appointed by the President.
The Constitution provides for the appointment of Regional Election Commissioners for each state.
The Election Commission's decisions are binding on the government but can be challenged in the High Court.
questions.explanation: Article 324 states the Election Commission comprises the Chief Election Commissioner and such number of Election Commissioners as the President may fix, currently two.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, a person convicted of which of the following offenses is disqualified from voting for a period of six years?
questions.explanation: Section 8A of the RPA, 1951 disqualifies a person convicted of corrupt practices for six years from the date of conviction.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act: 1. It provides for a uniform three-tier system of Panchayati Raj in all states. 2. The reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes is not applicable to the office of Chairperson. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect as states with population below 20 lakhs may not have intermediate tier; Statement 2 is incorrect as reservation applies to Chairperson offices.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu's local self-government, which of the following is correct regarding the 'District Planning Committee'?
options: It is chaired by the District Collector.
It is a constitutional body under Article 243ZD.
It has no role in consolidating plans of Panchayats and Municipalities.
Its members are entirely nominated by the State Government.
questions.explanation: Article 243ZD mandates the constitution of a District Planning Committee to consolidate plans prepared by Panchayats and Municipalities.

questions.question: The 'Model Code of Conduct' in India is enforced from the date of announcement of elections until the completion of the election process. Which of the following is its primary legal basis?
options: The Representation of the People Act, 1951
The Election Commission's plenary powers under the Constitution
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct derives its authority from the Election Commission's plenary powers under Article 324, not from a specific statute.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Constitution of India introduced the provision for 'free and fair elections' as a fundamental principle?
