questions.explanation: The phrase 'free and fair elections' is not explicitly mentioned in any amendment; it is implied through Article 324 and judicial interpretations.

questions.question: In the context of elections to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly, which of the following statements is correct regarding the 'Electoral Rolls'?
options: They are prepared and revised by the State Election Commission.
They are prepared by the Election Commission of India in consultation with the State Government.
They are revised every year with a qualifying date of 1st January.
They include only citizens who have attained the age of 21 years.
questions.explanation: Electoral rolls are revised annually with 1st January as the qualifying date, as per the Representation of the People Act, 1950.

questions.question: Which of the following is a unique feature of the 'First Past the Post' electoral system used in India for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections?
options: It ensures proportional representation of minorities.
The candidate with the highest number of votes wins, even if less than a majority.
Voters rank candidates in order of preference.
It requires a minimum voter turnout for the election to be valid.
questions.explanation: In the First Past the Post system, the candidate securing the highest number of votes, not necessarily a majority, is declared the winner.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Election Commission of India is correct?
options: It is a multi-member body with a fixed strength of three Election Commissioners.
The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only on the recommendation of the Supreme Court.
It has the power to conduct elections to the offices of the President and Vice-President.
Its decisions are binding on the government but not on political parties.
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the Election Commission with the superintendence of elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, a person convicted of which of the following offences is disqualified from voting?
questions.explanation: Section 16 of the RPA, 1951 disqualifies a person convicted of an offence punishable under Section 171E (bribery) or Section 171F (undue influence) of IPC, or under the Prevention of Corruption Act.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act: 1. It provides for reservation of seats for SCs and STs in every Panchayat. 2. It mandates a five-year term for all Panchayats. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Article 243D mandates reservation for SCs/STs, and Article 243E provides for a five-year term for Panchayats from the date of its first meeting.

questions.question: In the context of the Election Commission of India, what is the significance of the 'Model Code of Conduct'?
options: It is a legally binding document under the Constitution.
It comes into effect only after the notification of the election schedule.
It prohibits the use of government machinery for electioneering.
It is enforceable by the Supreme Court.
questions.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct is a set of guidelines agreed upon by political parties, and it prohibits the misuse of official machinery for campaigning, though it is not legally binding.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Constitution introduced the provision for 'free and fair elections' as a fundamental duty?
questions.explanation: No amendment has explicitly added 'free and fair elections' as a fundamental duty; it is implied through Article 326 and judicial interpretations.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, what is the minimum age for a person to contest elections to a Panchayat?
questions.explanation: Section 18 of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, prescribes the minimum age for contesting Panchayat elections as 21 years.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the 'Electoral Bond' scheme is correct?
options: It allows unlimited anonymous donations to political parties.
It was declared unconstitutional by the Supreme Court in 2024.
It is a form of cash donation to candidates.
It is available only to individuals, not companies.
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in 2024 struck down the Electoral Bond scheme as unconstitutional for violating the right to information under Article 19(1)(a).

questions.question: In the context of the 2013 Supreme Court judgment (Lily Thomas vs Union of India), which of the following is true regarding disqualification of MPs/MLAs?
options: Disqualification takes effect only after the appeal is exhausted.
Disqualification takes effect immediately upon conviction for an offence with a sentence of 2 years or more.
Disqualification applies only to offences under the Prevention of Corruption Act.
Disqualification can be waived by the President.
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court ruled that Section 8(3) of the RPA, 1951, disqualifies a convicted MP/MLA immediately upon conviction for an offence punishable with imprisonment of 2 years or more.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes the Election Commission of India from the State Election Commission under the Constitution?
options: The Election Commission of India is a multi-member body, while the State Election Commission is always a single-member body.
The State Election Commission is established by the Governor under Article 243K, whereas the Election Commission of India is established under Article 324.
The Election Commission of India conducts elections to Parliament and State Legislatures, while the State Election Commission conducts elections to local bodies only.
The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only by impeachment, while the State Election Commissioner can be removed by the President on the advice of the Governor.
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the EC for Union and State legislature elections; Article 243K establishes the SEC for Panchayat and Municipality elections.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the 'right to vote' in India: 1. It is a fundamental right under Article 326. 2. It is a constitutional right subject to statutory limitations. 3. It is a legal right under the Representation of the People Act, 1951. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The right to vote is a constitutional right (Article 326) and a legal right under RPA 1951, not a fundamental right.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, a person convicted of which of the following offences is disqualified from voting for a period of six years from the date of release?
questions.explanation: Section 8 of RPA 1951 disqualifies a person convicted of corrupt practice for six years after release.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid ground for declaring an election void under Section 100 of the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: That the returned candidate was not qualified to contest
That the election was not a free election due to bribery
That the result of the election was materially affected by the improper reception of votes
That the defeated candidate secured more valid votes than the returned candidate in a single round of counting
questions.explanation: The Act requires that the result be materially affected by an irregularity; a simple vote count difference is not a ground unless linked to an illegal practice.

questions.question: In the context of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, which of the following is correct regarding the reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes (SCs) in Panchayats?
options: Reservation applies only to the office of Chairperson, not to membership.
Reservation must be in proportion to the population of SCs in the Panchayat area.
Reservation ceases after the expiry of 25 years from the commencement of the Amendment.
Reservation is not applicable to Panchayats in Scheduled Areas.
questions.explanation: Article 243D mandates reservation for SCs in proportion to their population in the Panchayat area.

questions.question: A candidate for the office of President of India must be qualified to be elected as a member of which House?
questions.explanation: Article 58 requires the candidate to be qualified for election as a member of either House of Parliament.

questions.question: Under the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, 1994, what is the minimum age for a person to be eligible for election as a member of a Gram Panchayat?
questions.explanation: The Act prescribes 21 years as the minimum age for contesting Panchayat elections in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which of the following amendments to the Constitution introduced the concept of 'free and fair elections' as a basic feature of the Constitution?
options: None; the concept was read into Article 324 by judicial interpretation
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in various judgments (e.g., Indira Nehru Gandhi v. Raj Narain) held free and fair elections as part of the basic structure under Article 324.

questions.question: Consider the following: 1. Article 329(a) bars the courts from questioning the validity of any law relating to delimitation of constituencies. 2. Article 329(b) bars the courts from interfering in election matters except by an election petition. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Article 329(a) bars judicial review of delimitation laws; Article 329(b) mandates election petitions as the sole remedy for election disputes.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment introduced the provision for a State Election Commission for Panchayats?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) inserted Article 243K mandating a State Election Commission for Panchayats.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India: 1. The Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners are appointed by the President. 2. The conditions of service of the Chief Election Commissioner cannot be varied to his disadvantage after his appointment. 3. The Election Commission is a multi-member body consisting of a Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct as per Article 324 of the Constitution.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, which of the following is NOT a valid ground for disqualification of a Member of Parliament?
options: Failure to lodge election expenses account
Being a member of a banned political party
questions.explanation: Being a member of a banned political party is not a disqualification under the RPA, 1951; it may be under the Unlawful Activities Act.

questions.question: With reference to the Election Commission's powers, consider the following: 1. It can order a re-poll in a constituency due to large-scale rigging. 2. It can postpone an election due to a natural calamity. 3. It can cancel the election of a candidate found guilty of corrupt practices. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission can order re-poll or postpone elections, but cannot cancel a candidate's election; that is done by courts.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution provides for the electoral college for the election of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 54 defines the electoral college consisting of elected members of both Houses of Parliament and State Legislative Assemblies.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the 'right to vote' in India: 1. It is a constitutional right under Article 326. 2. It is a fundamental right under Article 19(1)(a). 3. It is a legal right under the Representation of the People Act. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Right to vote is a constitutional right under Article 326 and a legal right under RPA, but not a fundamental right.

questions.question: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, which of the following electoral offenses is/are punishable with imprisonment? 1. Personation 2. Bribery 3. Booth capturing. Select the correct answer using the codes below.
questions.explanation: Personation, bribery, and booth capturing are all electoral offenses punishable under the RPA, 1951.

questions.question: The Constitution (Seventy-third Amendment) Act, 1992, provides for the establishment of a State Election Commission for the conduct of elections to which of the following bodies?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment created a State Election Commission for Panchayats; the 74th Amendment for Municipalities.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the election of the Vice-President of India: 1. The Vice-President is elected by the members of both Houses of Parliament in a joint sitting. 2. The election is held by secret ballot. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is elected by members of both Houses voting together, not in a joint sitting; the election is by secret ballot.

questions.question: Regarding the anti-defection law under the Tenth Schedule of the Constitution, which of the following statements is correct?
options: The Speaker's decision on disqualification is final and cannot be challenged in any court.
The Governor can disqualify a member on the advice of the Council of Ministers.
The decision of the Speaker is subject to judicial review on limited grounds.
The Election Commission decides all disqualification petitions under the Tenth Schedule.
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in Kihoto Hollohon vs Zachilhu held that the Speaker's decision under the Tenth Schedule is subject to judicial review on limited grounds like violation of natural justice.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about the superintendence, direction and control of elections in India is/are correct?
1. The Election Commission of India conducts elections to both Parliament and State Legislatures.
2. Elections to local bodies are conducted by State Election Commissions.
3. The President can remove the Chief Election Commissioner only on the recommendation of the Supreme Court.
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests superintendence of elections to Parliament and State Legislatures in the ECI. State Election Commissions conduct local body elections (Art. 243K). CEC can be removed only by impeachment, not on SC recommendation.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the qualification for membership of a State Legislature:
1. A person must be at least 25 years of age to be a member of the Legislative Assembly.
2. A person must be at least 30 years of age to be a member of the Legislative Council.
3. A person must not hold any office of profit under the Government of India or any State.
Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Article 173 and relevant provisions: MLA minimum age 25, MLC minimum age 30, and holding an office of profit is a disqualification (Art. 191). All three statements are correct.

questions.question: With reference to election petitions in India, which of the following is correct?
options: Election petitions relating to Lok Sabha elections are heard directly by the Supreme Court.
Election petitions can be filed on any ground including the validity of any law relating to elections.
The High Court has jurisdiction to try election petitions for Parliamentary and Assembly constituencies.
Election petitions must be filed within 60 days from the date of election of the returned candidate.
questions.explanation: Under the Representation of the People Act, 1951, election petitions are heard by the High Court having jurisdiction over the constituency. They must be filed within 45 days. Grounds are limited.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ground for disqualification of a sitting MP or MLA under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Conviction for an offence listed under Section 8 of the Act.
Failure to lodge an account of election expenses within the prescribed time.
Being a director of a company that has a contract with the government.
Having been found guilty of corrupt practices by a High Court in an election petition.
questions.explanation: Being a director of a company with a government contract is not a ground for disqualification under the RP Act. The other three are valid grounds under Sections 8, 10A, and 8A respectively.

questions.question: Regarding the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in elections, which of the following statements is correct?
options: The MCC comes into effect from the date of dissolution of the House.
The MCC is enforced by the Election Commission under Article 324.
The MCC has statutory backing under the Representation of the People Act.
The MCC is applicable only to the ruling party and not to opposition parties.
questions.explanation: The MCC is a set of guidelines enforced by the EC under its plenary powers under Article 324. It comes into force from the date of announcement of election schedule, not dissolution. It has no statutory backing.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the voting rights of Non-Resident Indians (NRIs):
1. NRIs were first allowed to vote by way of the Representation of the People (Amendment) Act, 2010.
2. NRIs can vote in person at the polling station in their constituency of residence.
3. NRIs can now vote through postal ballot under certain conditions after the 2023 amendment.
Which of the statements are correct?
questions.explanation: The 2010 Act allowed NRIs to vote in person. The 2023 amendment introduced e-postal ballot facility for NRIs. All three statements are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the scheme of reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha?
options: Reservation is based on the proportion of SC/ST population in each state as per the last census.
Reservation is fixed at 15% for SC and 7.5% for ST seats irrespective of population.
Reservation is decided by the Election Commission after each census.
Reservation applies to all constituencies in the country uniformly.
questions.explanation: Articles 330 and 332 provide that seats are reserved in proportion to the SC/ST population in the state/UT based on the relevant census. It is not uniform across states.

questions.question: In the context of the election of the President of India, which of the following statements is correct?
options: The value of the vote of an MLA is determined by dividing the total population of the state by the number of elected MLAs.
The value of the vote of an MP is uniform and is determined by dividing the total value of all MLA votes by the number of MPs.
The election is conducted by the Election Commission using the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote.
All the above statements are correct.
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct as per Article 55 and the Presidential and Vice-Presidential Elections Act. The formulae are as stated.

questions.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the delimitation of constituencies:
1. Delimitation is the process of redrawing boundaries of constituencies based on the latest census.
2. The Delimitation Commission's orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.
3. The number of seats in the Lok Sabha has been frozen until 2026 based on the 1971 census.
Which of the statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Delimitation is based on census data. The orders of the Delimitation Commission are final and cannot be questioned in court (Art. 329(a)). Lok Sabha seats are frozen until 2026 by the 42nd Amendment based on 1971 census. All three correct.

questions.question: Which of the following provisions regarding elections to the Panchayats is NOT correct under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act?
options: Reservation of seats for SC/ST in proportion to their population
State Election Commission conducts elections to Panchayats
Direct elections to all members of Panchayats at all levels
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment provides for direct elections to Gram Panchayat and intermediate levels, but members of District Panchayat are indirectly elected by the intermediate Panchayat members.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India: 1. It is a multi-member body with a Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners. 2. The Chief Election Commissioner can be removed only on the ground of proved misbehaviour or incapacity. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is a multi-member body since 1993, and the Chief Election Commissioner has the same security of tenure as a Supreme Court judge, removable only by impeachment.

questions.question: Under Article 324 of the Constitution, the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to which of the following bodies is vested in the Election Commission of India?
options: Parliament, State Legislatures, and Local Bodies
Parliament, State Legislatures, and Offices of President and Vice-President
questions.explanation: Article 324 covers elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President, but not local bodies which are under State Election Commissions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid ground for disqualification of a candidate from contesting elections under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Conviction for a crime involving moral turpitude with a sentence of two years
Conviction for any offence under the Prevention of Corruption Act
questions.explanation: Section 8 of the RPA, 1951 disqualifies a person convicted under the Prevention of Corruption Act, regardless of sentence, while other offences require a minimum sentence of two years.

questions.question: In the context of the Preamble of the Indian Constitution, which of the following principles is directly reflected in the provision for universal adult suffrage?
questions.explanation: Universal adult suffrage ensures that every adult citizen has the right to vote, which is the essence of a democratic polity as stated in the Preamble.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the State Election Commission in Tamil Nadu?
options: Delimitation of wards for local bodies
Conducting elections to the Legislative Assembly
questions.explanation: Elections to the Legislative Assembly are conducted by the Election Commission of India, not the State Election Commission, which only handles local body elections.

questions.question: Under the 97th Constitutional Amendment Act, which of the following is a mandatory requirement for cooperative societies regarding elections?
options: Elections must be conducted by the State Election Commission
The term of the elected board is fixed at five years
Elections must be held before the expiry of the term
questions.explanation: The 97th Amendment added Part IXB, which mandates that elections to cooperative societies must be conducted before the expiry of the term, but does not specify the conducting body or reservation details.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the 'Election Petition' under the Representation of the People Act, 1951 is correct?
options: It can be filed only in the Supreme Court
It must be filed within 45 days of the election result
It can be filed on the ground of corrupt practice
The petitioner must deposit a security of Rs. 25,000
questions.explanation: Election petitions can be filed in the High Court on grounds like corrupt practice (Section 100), and the limitation period is 30 days, not 45.

questions.question: In the context of the 'Model Code of Conduct' during elections, which of the following actions by a ruling party is considered a violation?
options: Announcing a new welfare scheme in the election manifesto
Using government vehicles for election campaigning
Criticizing opposition policies in a public rally
Distributing freebies promised in the manifesto
questions.explanation: The Model Code prohibits the use of government resources, including vehicles, for election campaigning, while announcing schemes in manifestos is allowed but subject to scrutiny.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the concept of 'One Nation, One Election' as proposed in recent debates?
options: Simultaneous elections for Lok Sabha and all State Assemblies
Uniform electoral rolls for all elections in India
Single election for President and Prime Minister
Elections conducted by a single commission for all tiers
questions.explanation: The proposal aims to synchronize the election cycles of Lok Sabha and all State Legislative Assemblies to reduce frequent elections and administrative costs.

questions.question: Under Article 324 of the Indian Constitution, the superintendence, direction, and control of elections to which of the following bodies vests in the Election Commission?
options: Parliament, State Legislatures, and Offices of President and Vice-President
questions.explanation: Article 324 covers elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President, but not local bodies.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) in India: 1. It is enforceable by law under the Representation of the People Act, 1951. 2. It comes into effect from the date of announcement of the election schedule. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The MCC is not legally enforceable; it is a voluntary agreement. It comes into effect from the date of announcement of the election schedule.

questions.question: Which of the following is a ground for disqualification of a Member of Parliament under the Representation of the People Act, 1951?
options: Conviction for insulting the Indian national flag
Failure to lodge election expenses within the prescribed time
Being a director of a government company
Holding an office of profit under the state government
questions.explanation: Failure to lodge election expenses within the prescribed time is a ground for disqualification under Section 10A of the RPA, 1951.

questions.question: In the context of the 73rd Constitutional Amendment, which of the following statements about the election of Panchayat members is correct?
options: The State Election Commission conducts elections to Panchayats, and the superintendence of the Election Commission of India does not apply.
The Election Commission of India conducts all Panchayat elections directly.
Panchayat elections are conducted by the State Election Commission but under the supervision of the Election Commission of India.
Panchayat elections are conducted by the District Collector as per the State Election Commission's guidelines.
questions.explanation: Under Article 243K, the State Election Commission is vested with the superintendence of Panchayat elections, independent of the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the electoral system for the Rajya Sabha?
options: State legislative assemblies as the electorate
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha members are indirectly elected by state legislative assemblies, not directly by the people.

questions.question: Which of the following grounds for disqualification from being a member of either House of Parliament is explicitly mentioned in Article 102 of the Constitution of India, without requiring any law to be enacted?
options: Conviction for an offence with 2+ years imprisonment
Guilty of a corrupt practice in an election
questions.explanation: Article 102(1)(c) directly disqualifies an undischarged insolvent. The other disqualifications are statutory under the Representation of the People Act, 1951.
